6-exec insider cluster too recent for scanner's insider detection lag — broadest C-suite cluster buy in years at 8x fwd P/E
Scanner doesn't model geopolitical ton-mile impacts on tankers — VLCC rates at all-time highs locked in at $107K/day
Scanner filters may penalize recent acquisitions (Celestial AI) — cheapest AI semi at 17x fwd P/E with 5-year AWS deal
First profitable year wasn't in scanner's trailing data yet — 12x fwd P/E for 43% grower, prediction market bill catalyst
RPA companies may be filtered out as legacy tech — first GAAP profit, 83% margins, agentic AI pivot at 12.4x fwd P/E
Activist situations require real-time monitoring — $500M buyback + Engaged Capital pushing strategic alternatives
Mid-cap defense not in scanner's primary universe — $1.45B hypersonic contract, Golden Dome vendor
Recent IPO may be excluded from scanner screens — 92% growth, 75.7% GM, AI connectivity leader at 54% drawdown

catalysts_general: Largest US LNG exporter. Structural supply gap from Hormuz conflict. Near ATH risk. (Tier 2: news)

Discovery: Strait of Hormuz closure and Iranian missile strikes on Qatar's LNG facilities (12.8M tpa offline for 3-5 years) create a structural supply gap that directly benefits the largest US LNG exporter; up 45% YTD with 95% capacity contracted for 10 years and FY2025 revenue +27% YoY to $20B.
value_picks.analyst_take: Largest US LNG exporter benefiting from structural supply gap, Qatar facility damage catalyst

catalysts_general: FY2025 revenue $22.94B, net income $1.58B. Google AI partnership. Consensus Strong Buy $137-184 target. Q4 profit miss. (Tier 1: Fortune, Bloomberg)

Discovery: Down 60% from 52-week high ($199 to ~$79) near 52-week lows despite spectacular fundamentals — full-year revenue surged 36% YoY to $22.9B, net income jumped 260% to $1.6B, all three segments profitable (Shopee, SeaMoney, Garena); Q4 revenue beat ($6.9B vs $6.49B expected) but EPS miss ($0.63 vs $0.80) triggered indiscriminate selling; forward P/E ~22x with PEG well below 1 for a triple-engine Singapore-based company (NOT Chinese ADR); 29 analysts on Buy, zero Sells, consensus target $141 (78% upside); Q1 2026 earnings in ~60 days is the catalyst to prove sustained profitability.
value_picks.analyst_take: Fastest-growing large-cap e-commerce at 15.5x fwd P/E with 38% revenue growth and Google AI partnership

value_picks.analyst_take: Coterra merger closes April 1, combined entity = largest Delaware Basin operator, sub-$40 breakeven vs $108 Brent
